message.content:
{
  "homework": {
    "sessionInformation": {
      "sessionNumber": "1",
      "sessionTitle": "Number System - Session 1",
      "subject": "Mathematics",
      "grade": "Class IX",
      "difficultyLevel": "Balanced",
      "learningPace": "Balanced",
      "estimatedHomeworkDuration": "45 minutes"
    },
    "homework": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "type": "MCQ",
        "title": "Identifying Rational Numbers",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_01"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Rational Numbers", "Definition of Rational Numbers"],
        "difficulty": "Easy",
        "marks": 2,
        "estimatedTime": "5 minutes",
        "instructions": "Select the correct option that represents a rational number.",
        "question": "Which of the following is a rational number?",
        "options": [
          "√2",
          "π",
          "0.75",
          "∛3"
        ],
        "answerSpace": "Write the correct option letter here: ____",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "C (0.75)"
      },
      {
        "id": 2,
        "type": "Fill in the blanks",
        "title": "Properties of Rational Numbers",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_01", "LO_02"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Rational Numbers", "Closure Property"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 3,
        "estimatedTime": "8 minutes",
        "instructions": "Fill in the blanks with appropriate terms based on the properties of rational numbers.",
        "question": "The sum of two rational numbers is always a ______ number. The product of two non-zero rational numbers is always a ______ number.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write your answers here: ______, ______",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "rational, rational"
      },
      {
        "id": 3,
        "type": "Numerical Problem",
        "title": "Representing Rational Numbers on a Number Line",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_02"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Representation of Rational Numbers", "Number Line"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 4,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Draw a number line and mark the position of the given rational numbers. Show your steps for finding the positions.",
        "question": "Represent the following rational numbers on a number line: -3/2 and 5/4.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Draw your number line in the space provided below.",
        "visualRequirement": "Draw a horizontal line with arrows at both ends. Mark integers from -2 to 3. Divide segments into appropriate parts to locate -1.5 and 1.25.",
        "expectedResponse": "A correctly drawn number line showing -1.5 (between -1 and -2) and 1.25 (between 1 and 2)."
      },
      {
        "id": 4,
        "type": "Short Answer",
        "title": "Difference between Rational and Irrational Numbers",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_01"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Rational Numbers", "Irrational Numbers"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 3,
        "estimatedTime": "7 minutes",
        "instructions": "Explain the difference between rational and irrational numbers with one example of each.",
        "question": "What is the main difference between a rational number and an irrational number? Give one example of each.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write your explanation here:",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "A rational number can be expressed as p/q where p and q are integers and q≠0 (e.g., 1/2). An irrational number cannot be expressed in this form and has a non-terminating, non-repeating decimal expansion (e.g., √2)."
      },
      {
        "id": 5,
        "type": "Real-life Application",
        "title": "Rational Numbers in Daily Life",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_03"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Application of Rational Numbers"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 4,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Identify rational numbers in the given real-life scenarios and explain why they are rational.",
        "question": "A recipe requires 3/4 cup of sugar. A car travels 150 km using 12 liters of fuel. Identify the rational numbers in these statements and explain why they are rational.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write your answers here:",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "3/4 is rational because it is a ratio of integers. 150/12 (or 12.5) is rational because it can be expressed as a fraction of integers."
      },
      {
        "id": 6,
        "type": "Assertion Reason",
        "title": "Conceptual Understanding of Rational Numbers",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_01", "LO_02"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Rational Numbers", "Properties"],
        "difficulty": "Hard",
        "marks": 4,
        "estimatedTime": "8 minutes",
        "instructions": "Read the Assertion (A) and Reason (R) carefully. Choose the correct option.",
        "question": "Assertion (A): Every integer is a rational number.\nReason (R): An integer can be expressed in the form p/q where q is not zero.",
        "options": [
          "Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A",
          "Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A",
          "A is true but R is false",
          "A is false but R is true"
        ],
        "answerSpace": "Select the correct option: ____",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A."
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "totalQuestions": 6,
      "totalMarks": 20,
      "estimatedCompletionTime": "48 minutes",
      "learningOutcomesCovered": [
        "LO_01: Define and identify rational numbers",
        "LO_02: Represent rational numbers on a number line and apply closure properties",
        "LO_03: Apply rational numbers in real-life contexts"
      ],
      "topicsCovered": [
        "Rational Numbers",
        "Irrational Numbers",
        "Representation of Rational Numbers",
        "Closure Property",
        "Application of Rational Numbers"
      ],
      "subtopicsCovered": [
        "Definition of Rational Numbers",
        "Number Line Representation",
        "Difference between Rational and Irrational",
        "Real-life Scenarios"
      ],
      "taskDistribution": {
        "MCQ": 1,
        "Fill in the blanks": 1,
        "Numerical Problem": 1,
        "Short Answer": 1,
        "Real-life Application": 1,
        "Assertion Reason": 1
      },
      "homeExperimentIncluded": false,
      "parentEngagementIncluded": true
    }
  }
}
